theory.intro: Two lines are **perpendicular** when they meet (or cross) to form a right angle of $90^{\circ}$.
facts: Perpendicular lines meet at exactly $90^{\circ}$ — a square corner.
A small **square** drawn at the meeting point marks the right angle.
The symbol $\perp$ means 'is perpendicular to', e.g. $AB \perp CD$.
steps: Look at the angle where the lines meet.
If it is $90^{\circ}$ (square mark), the lines are perpendicular.
